priority_1_intervention.name: scaffolded_readout_vs_baseline_intervention
priority_1_intervention.expected_brain_side_signal: preserve family patch separation while improving readout transport
priority_1_intervention.why_now: directly targets the highest-severity gap: object_to_readout_compatibility

priority_2_intervention.name: reasoning_slice_transport_intervention
priority_2_intervention.expected_brain_side_signal: shared reasoning slice should help without reverting to a global central loop
priority_2_intervention.why_now: ties unified reasoning to the current best P3 operator

brain_side_expectations.object_family_patch_falsification: family patch separation remains high while readout transport improves
brain_side_expectations.reasoning_slice_falsification: conditioned-entry shared-slice explains the gain better than a fully shared global loop

verdict.core_answer: Priority 1 and 2 interventions are jointly promising and should be executed before stress and relation interventions.
verdict.next_engineering_target: run these two interventions first and use their outcome as the gate for the next theorem survival pass.
